generated_oml:
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-definition LiDAR sensor retrofitted onto the vehicle chassis, capturing raw point‑cloud bytes"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARRawBytes
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor capturing raw vehicle speed and acceleration bytes"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateRawData
]

instance LiDARRawBytes : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateRawData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance LiDARRawData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized raw LiDAR point‑cloud data received via UDP"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARRawBytes
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized vehicle speed and acceleration data received via UDP"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateRawData
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation component that serializes raw sensor bytes, aligns the raw message types to ISO 23247 schema, and performs necessary unit conversions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARRawData, VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data Monitoring service providing a user interface that visualizes real‑time vehicle state, projected path, detected object tracks and allows saving and reporting"
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightVehicleState, InsightProjectedPath, InsightObjectTrack, ActionGenerateReport
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation Offloading service that offloads ACC processing to remote compute, determines the lead vehicle, calculates desired speed, and returns speed command"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionSetDesiredSpeed
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote Management service that enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking via UI"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionEnableACC, ActionDisableACC, ActionEmergencyBrake
]

instance InsightVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time vehicle state including speed and acceleration"
]

instance InsightProjectedPath : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected path of the vehicle based on ACC logic"
]

instance InsightObjectTrack : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected object tracks around the vehicle from point‑cloud clustering"
]

instance ActionGenerateReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates and saves a report of collected data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionSetDesiredSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sets desired speed command returned by remote ACC computation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActionEnableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables Adaptive Cruise Control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionDisableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Disables Adaptive Cruise Control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionEmergencyBrake : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers emergency braking command"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands from the Virtual cockpit’s Control Panel (for example: enable/disable Adaptive Cruise Control, modify desired speed, trigger emergency braking) are encapsulated by the Control FE and sent over UDP through the Wi‑Fi link to the vehicle’s Actuation FE. The Actuation FE validates and authenticates each command before forwarding it to the vehicle’s motor, steering, or braking actuators. Feedback from the actuators (e.g., actual speed, braking status) is returned along the same path, enabling the Digital Twin to confirm command execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin system requires a shared notion of time across physical and digital components to support computation offloading. Standards mandate clock synchronization tighter than 100 ms for safety‑critical vehicular applications; due to hardware limitations, the design targets a 100 ms absolute time‑accuracy budget. This requirement governs the temporal granularity of service interactions, ensuring that data exchanges and control commands are timestamped with at least 100 ms precision so that physical sensors, actuators, and digital processing elements operate in a temporally coherent manner."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin system follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping software components to Functional Elements (FEs) and implementing each FE as an individual ROS 2 package. These packages encapsulate one or more ROS 2 nodes that provide the intended functionality. The orchestration of the system is thus defined by the entity structure: User Entity (FE split into visualization and control panels), Digital Twin Entity (data management and control mechanisms), and Physical Twin Entity (resource handling and general automotive functions). The design deviates from the reference architecture by introducing the Physical Twin Entity, which consolidates compute, sensor, and actuator resources and supports automotive‑specific operations. Coordination occurs across these entities via ROS 2 messaging, with each FE’s execution context governed by its deployed hardware component. The architecture also includes a Data Storage component that lacks a naturally aligned FE, indicating an explicit allocation decision in the functional mapping."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Network infrastructure consists of a dual‑layer architecture: intra‑software communication leverages ROS 2 over a local serial bus or Ethernet, while inter‑entity communication uses Wi‑Fi links carrying UDP packets. The system must sustain a maximum round‑trip latency of 100 ms from the vehicle to the digital twin, as specified by requirement R7, to support timely visualisation and control. Bandwidth requirements are driven by the LiDAR raw point cloud data stream (typically tens of megabytes per second) and vehicle state updates, though explicit limits are not documented. No redundancy or high‑availability topology is specified in the current documentation, nor are specific security protocols (e.g., TLS) described."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Compliant with ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing (Part 2 – Reference Architecture), adopting an entity‑based reference architecture comprising Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Functional Entities within each component may be implemented using OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA). The demonstration is published on a public GitHub repository (https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin) and a YouTube video (https://youtu.be/Rv-sJJQc3WU), illustrating the interoperability of the reference model with standard OPC UA services."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Compliance with ISO 15622:2018 – The Digital Twin system implements Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) following the performance requirements and test procedures defined in ISO 15622. The system must meet the specified vehicle‑dynamic performance, collision‑avoidance characteristics, and satisfy the integrity tests of this standard, thereby ensuring functional safety of ACC operation. In addition, network requirements are dictated by 3GPP TS 22.185: the Digital Twin must maintain vehicle‑to‑vehicle latency of no more than 100 ms for V2X data exchange, ensuring timely delivery of situational awareness data critical for safe ACC behavior."
]
